system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer the user's question helpfully and specifically. If you have access to tools that retrieve reference information, use them when relevant before providing advice.
turn1_user_message: Quick travel question. I'm a US passport holder heading to Vietnam in two weeks for a 12-day trip — Hanoi and Ha Long Bay, just tourism. Do I need a visa for Vietnam? My passport is valid for another 3 years.
turn2_user_message: Perfect, thanks. Can you write me a quick trip-prep checklist for the departure airport — what documents to bring, what to expect at check-in and at Hanoi immigration, and anything I should print? I travel light and want to make sure I'm not missing anything.
response_turn1:
Great question! Here's what you need to know:

## Vietnam Visa Requirements for US Citizens

**Yes, you will need a visa.** US passport holders are **not** among the nationalities that qualify for Vietnam's visa exemption program.

You have two main options:

### 1. **E-Visa (Recommended for your trip)**
- **Duration:** Valid for up to 90 days, single or multiple entry
- **Processing time:** Typically **3 business days**
- **Cost:** ~$25
- **How:** Apply online at the official Vietnamese immigration portal (search for Vietnam e-visa official site — be careful of third-party sites that charge extra fees)
- This is the easiest and most common option for US tourists

### 2. **Visa on Arrival**
- Available at major international airports (Hanoi's Noi Bai Airport qualifies)
- Usually requires a pre-approved invitation letter through an agency
- Generally more hassle than the e-visa these days

## Passport Validity
Vietnam requires your passport to be valid for **at least 6 months** beyond your entry date. With 3 years remaining, you're well in the clear. ✅

## Key Reminders
- **Two weeks is enough time** to get an e-visa, but don't delay — apply soon
- Make sure to print a copy of your approved e-visa to carry with you
- Your 12-day tourism trip fits well within the e-visa allowance

**Bottom line: Apply for the e-visa ASAP and you'll be all set.** Enjoy Hanoi and Ha Long Bay! 🇻🇳
response_turn2:
# ✈️ Vietnam Trip-Prep Checklist: Airport & Arrival

---

## 📄 Documents to Bring (Physical Copies)

- [ ] **US Passport** (valid 3+ years — you're good)
- [ ] **Printed e-visa approval letter** (don't rely solely on your phone)
- [ ] **Round-trip flight itinerary / booking confirmation**
- [ ] **Hotel reservation confirmations** (Hanoi + Ha Long Bay)
- [ ] **Travel insurance policy summary** (highly recommended for Vietnam; some immigration officers may ask)
- [ ] **Emergency contact info** — US Embassy in Hanoi address & phone number

### On Your Phone (Backup Copies)
- [ ] Photos/PDFs of everything above
- [ ] Offline copy of your e-visa
- [ ] Screenshot of your accommodation addresses in Vietnamese (helpful for taxis too)

---

## 🛫 At Your Departure Airport (US Side)

### Check-in Counter
- Present **passport + flight booking confirmation**
- Airline staff may ask to see your **e-visa** to confirm you're authorized to enter Vietnam — have it ready
- Confirm baggage allowance (varies by airline/route)
- Ask for your **boarding passes for all legs** if you have connections

### US Security / TSA
- Nothing Vietnam-specific here, just standard procedure
- Keep your passport accessible as your ID

---

## 🛬 Arriving at Noi Bai Airport (Hanoi)

### What to Expect at Immigration

1. **Before you land** — Flight attendants may hand out an **arrival/departure card** to fill out (name, passport number, address in Vietnam, flight number). Bring a pen ✏️
2. **Immigration queue** — Join the line for **foreign passports**
3. **At the counter, present:**
   - Passport
   - Printed e-visa
   - Completed arrival card (if applicable)
4. **Biometrics** — Expect fingerprint scanning and a photo taken at the booth
5. **Passport stamp** — Officer stamps your entry; double-check the dates are correct before walking away
6. **Baggage claim** → **Customs** (usually just walk through the green "nothing to declare" lane for standard tourism)

### Typical processing time
- 20–45 minutes depending on how many flights land simultaneously

---

## 🔑 Small But Important Details

| Item | Why |
|---|---|
| **Pen in carry-on** | For arrival card on the plane |
| **Passport photos (2 extras)** | Backup in case of any document issues |
| **Local currency** | ATMs are available at Noi Bai, but having ~$20–50 USD cash as a fallback is smart |
| **SIM card or eSIM plan** | Grab a tourist SIM at the airport arrivals hall or set up an eSIM before departure — you'll want data for maps, Grab (ride-hailing app), and translation |
| **Accommodation address in Vietnamese** | Show it to your taxi/Grab driver to avoid confusion |

---

## ⚠️ Common Mistakes to Avoid

- **Don't skip printing the e-visa** — phone screens crack, batteries die, airport Wi-Fi fails
- **Don't forget to check your entry stamp** — make sure the allowed stay dates cover your full 12 days
- **Don't exchange money at the airport at the first booth you see** — rates are poor; use an ATM instead or exchange a small amount and get a better rate in the city
- **Don't book a taxi from the arrivals touts** — use the official airport taxi counters or pre-book a Grab car

---

## 📋 Final Pre-Departure Mini Checklist

- [ ] E-visa applied for and approved
- [ ] All documents printed and in a folder
- [ ] Backups saved to phone
- [ ] Travel insurance purchased
- [ ] Accommodation booked and addresses saved
- [ ] Notify your bank you're traveling to Vietnam (to avoid card blocks)
- [ ] Download offline maps for Hanoi and Ha Long Bay area
- [ ] Download the **Grab** app

---

You're in great shape with two weeks to prepare. Safe travels and enjoy the phở in Hanoi! 🍜
